Torii gate and kagura hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Motosu City|Q844047|WD=Q844047}} Neo Nōgō 162 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, debated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Other names = Hakusan Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nōgō
Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} [[File:Shinto shrine Kumanohakusangongen 2010-4-4.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Okumiya (April 2010)]] '''Nōgō Hakusan Shrine''' (のうごうはくさんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Neo Nōgō, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture. Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) * Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/motosusi/nougou.html |title=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Gifu Prefecture–designated cultural properties * Japanese bronze mirror (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/wakyou.html |title=Japanese Bronze Mirror |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh masks (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noumenn.html |title=Noh Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen costumes (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noukyougennshou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Costumes |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Bronze temple bell (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/bonnshou.html |title=Bronze Bell |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen manuscripts (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenseki/motosusi/noukyou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Manuscripts |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Offering vessels (Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/motosusi/kumotuki.html |title=Offering Vessels |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Motosu City–designated cultural properties * Buddhist statues{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kaiga/ |title=Paintings and Sculptures |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Lotus Sutra written in gold on indigo paper{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/tenseki/ |title=Manuscripts |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Golden Light Sutra written in gold on indigo paper * Waniguchi gong{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kougei/ |title=Craftworks |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh flute === Access === ; Main shrine * The nearest public transportation is about 10 km from {{ill|lt=Tarumi Station|Q7686918|WD=Q7686918}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Q7686917|WD=Q7686917}} of {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718}}. * Take the Motosu City Bus Nōgō Line and get off at the “Nōgō” bus stop; about a 5-minute walk. === Other === There is a theory that this shrine corresponds to “Hananaga Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} under {{ill|lt=Ōno District, Mino Province|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757}}. Although Nōgō Hakusan Shrine is located in Motosu City (formerly {{ill|lt=Neo Village|Q11536289|WD=Q11536289}} of {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331}}), until {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 29 ({{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|WD=Q7846}}) it belonged to {{ill|lt=Nishi-Neo Village|Q11628785|WD=Q11628785}} of Ōno District. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nougou Hakusan Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:白山神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:本巣市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, debated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Hakusan Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nōgō
Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} [[File:Shinto shrine Kumanohakusangongen 2010-4-4.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Okumiya (April 2010)]] '''Nōgō Hakusan Shrine''' (のうごうはくさんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Neo Nōgō, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture. Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) * Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/motosusi/nougou.html |title=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Gifu Prefecture–designated cultural properties * Japanese bronze mirror (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/wakyou.html |title=Japanese Bronze Mirror |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh masks (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noumenn.html |title=Noh Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen costumes (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noukyougennshou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Costumes |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Bronze temple bell (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/bonnshou.html |title=Bronze Bell |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen manuscripts (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenseki/motosusi/noukyou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Manuscripts |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Offering vessels (Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/motosusi/kumotuki.html |title=Offering Vessels |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Motosu City–designated cultural properties * Buddhist statues{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kaiga/ |title=Paintings and Sculptures |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Lotus Sutra written in gold on indigo paper{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/tenseki/ |title=Manuscripts |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Golden Light Sutra written in gold on indigo paper * Waniguchi gong{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kougei/ |title=Craftworks |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh flute === Access === ; Main shrine * The nearest public transportation is about 10 km from {{ill|lt=Tarumi Station|Q7686918|WD=Q7686918|1=Tarumi Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Q7686917|WD=Q7686917|1=Tarumi Line}} of {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. * Take the Motosu City Bus Nōgō Line and get off at the “Nōgō” bus stop; about a 5-minute walk. === Other === There is a theory that this shrine corresponds to “Hananaga Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Ōno District, Mino Province|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|1=Ōno district}}. Although Nōgō Hakusan Shrine is located in Motosu City (formerly {{ill|lt=Neo Village|Q11536289|WD=Q11536289|1=Neo}} of {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}}), until {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 29 ({{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|WD=Q7846|1=1896}}) it belonged to {{ill|lt=Nishi-Neo Village|Q11628785|WD=Q11628785|1=Nishineo}} of Ōno District. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nougou Hakusan Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:白山神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:本巣市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) * Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/motosusi/nougou.html |title=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Gifu Prefecture–designated cultural properties * Japanese bronze mirror (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/wakyou.html |title=Japanese Bronze Mirror |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh masks (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noumenn.html |title=Noh Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen costumes (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noukyougennshou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Costumes |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Bronze temple bell (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/bonnshou.html |title=Bronze Bell |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen manuscripts (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenseki/motosusi/noukyou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Manuscripts |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Offering vessels (Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/motosusi/kumotuki.html |title=Offering Vessels |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Motosu City–designated cultural properties * Buddhist statues{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kaiga/ |title=Paintings and Sculptures |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Lotus Sutra written in gold on indigo paper{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/tenseki/ |title=Manuscripts |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Golden Light Sutra written in gold on indigo paper * Waniguchi gong{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kougei/ |title=Craftworks |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh flute === Access === ; Main shrine * The nearest public transportation is about 10 km from {{ill|lt=Tarumi Station|Q7686918|WD=Q7686918|1=Tarumi Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Q7686917|WD=Q7686917|1=Tarumi Line}} of {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. * Take the Motosu City Bus Nōgō Line and get off at the “Nōgō” bus stop; about a 5-minute walk. === Other === There is a theory that this shrine corresponds to “Hananaga Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Ōno District, Mino Province|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|1=Ōno district}}. Although Nōgō Hakusan Shrine is located in Motosu City (formerly {{ill|lt=Neo Village|Q11536289|WD=Q11536289|1=Neo}} of {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}}), until {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 29 ({{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|WD=Q7846|1=1896}}) it belonged to {{ill|lt=Nishi-Neo Village|Q11628785|WD=Q11628785|1=Nishineo}} of Ōno District. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nougou Hakusan Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:白山神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:本巣市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, debated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Hakusan Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nōgō
Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} [[File:Shinto shrine Kumanohakusangongen 2010-4-4.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Okumiya (April 2010)]] '''Nōgō Hakusan Shrine''' (のうごうはくさんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Neo Nōgō, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture. Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) * Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/motosusi/nougou.html |title=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Gifu Prefecture–designated cultural properties * Japanese bronze mirror (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/wakyou.html |title=Japanese Bronze Mirror |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh masks (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noumenn.html |title=Noh Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen costumes (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noukyougennshou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Costumes |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Bronze temple bell (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/bonnshou.html |title=Bronze Bell |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen manuscripts (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenseki/motosusi/noukyou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Manuscripts |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Offering vessels (Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/motosusi/kumotuki.html |title=Offering Vessels |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Motosu City–designated cultural properties * Buddhist statues{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kaiga/ |title=Paintings and Sculptures |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Lotus Sutra written in gold on indigo paper{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/tenseki/ |title=Manuscripts |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Golden Light Sutra written in gold on indigo paper * Waniguchi gong{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kougei/ |title=Craftworks |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh flute === Access === ; Main shrine * The nearest public transportation is about 10 km from {{ill|lt=Tarumi Station|Q7686918|WD=Q7686918|1=Tarumi Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Q7686917|WD=Q7686917|1=Tarumi Line}} of {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. * Take the Motosu City Bus Nōgō Line and get off at the “Nōgō” bus stop; about a 5-minute walk. === Other === There is a theory that this shrine corresponds to “Hananaga Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Ōno District, Mino Province|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|1=Ōno district}}. Although Nōgō Hakusan Shrine is located in Motosu City (formerly {{ill|lt=Neo Village|Q11536289|WD=Q11536289|1=Neo}} of {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}}), until {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 29 ({{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|WD=Q7846|1=1896}}) it belonged to {{ill|lt=Nishi-Neo Village|Q11628785|WD=Q11628785|1=Nishineo}} of Ōno District. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:白山神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:本巣市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

{{wikidata link|Q11612076}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Nōgō Hakusan Shrine |Image = [[Image:Shinto shrine Hakusan in Nougou 2010-4-4.JPG|270px|]]
Torii gate and kagura hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Motosu City|Q844047|WD=Q844047|1=Motosu, Gifu}} Neo Nōgō 162 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, debated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Hakusan Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nōgō
Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} [[File:Shinto shrine Kumanohakusangongen 2010-4-4.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Okumiya (April 2010)]] '''Nōgō Hakusan Shrine''' (のうごうはくさんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Neo Nōgō, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture. Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) * Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/motosusi/nougou.html |title=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Gifu Prefecture–designated cultural properties * Japanese bronze mirror (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/wakyou.html |title=Japanese Bronze Mirror |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh masks (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noumenn.html |title=Noh Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen costumes (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noukyougennshou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Costumes |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Bronze temple bell (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/bonnshou.html |title=Bronze Bell |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen manuscripts (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenseki/motosusi/noukyou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Manuscripts |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Offering vessels (Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/motosusi/kumotuki.html |title=Offering Vessels |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Motosu City–designated cultural properties * Buddhist statues{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kaiga/ |title=Paintings and Sculptures |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Lotus Sutra written in gold on indigo paper{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/tenseki/ |title=Manuscripts |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Golden Light Sutra written in gold on indigo paper * Waniguchi gong{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kougei/ |title=Craftworks |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh flute === Access === ; Main shrine * The nearest public transportation is about 10 km from {{ill|lt=Tarumi Station|Q7686918|WD=Q7686918|1=Tarumi Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Q7686917|WD=Q7686917|1=Tarumi Line}} of {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. * Take the Motosu City Bus Nōgō Line and get off at the “Nōgō” bus stop; about a 5-minute walk. === Other === There is a theory that this shrine corresponds to “Hananaga Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Ōno District, Mino Province|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|1=Ōno district}}. Although Nōgō Hakusan Shrine is located in Motosu City (formerly {{ill|lt=Neo Village|Q11536289|WD=Q11536289|1=Neo}} of {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}}), until {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 29 ({{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|WD=Q7846|1=1896}}) it belonged to {{ill|lt=Nishi-Neo Village|Q11628785|WD=Q11628785|1=Nishineo}} of Ōno District. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:白山神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:本巣市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) * Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/motosusi/nougou.html |title=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Gifu Prefecture–designated cultural properties * Japanese bronze mirror (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/wakyou.html |title=Japanese Bronze Mirror |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh masks (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noumenn.html |title=Noh Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen costumes (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noukyougennshou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Costumes |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Bronze temple bell (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/bonnshou.html |title=Bronze Bell |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen manuscripts (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenseki/motosusi/noukyou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Manuscripts |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Offering vessels (Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/motosusi/kumotuki.html |title=Offering Vessels |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Motosu City–designated cultural properties * Buddhist statues{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kaiga/ |title=Paintings and Sculptures |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Lotus Sutra written in gold on indigo paper{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/tenseki/ |title=Manuscripts |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Golden Light Sutra written in gold on indigo paper * Waniguchi gong{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kougei/ |title=Craftworks |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh flute === Access === ; Main shrine * The nearest public transportation is about 10 km from {{ill|lt=Tarumi Station|Q7686918|WD=Q7686918|1=Tarumi Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Q7686917|WD=Q7686917|1=Tarumi Line}} of {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. * Take the Motosu City Bus Nōgō Line and get off at the “Nōgō” bus stop; about a 5-minute walk. === Other === There is a theory that this shrine corresponds to “Hananaga Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Ōno District, Mino Province|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|1=Ōno district}}. Although Nōgō Hakusan Shrine is located in Motosu City (formerly {{ill|lt=Neo Village|Q11536289|WD=Q11536289|1=Neo}} of {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}}), until {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 29 ({{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|WD=Q7846|1=1896}}) it belonged to {{ill|lt=Nishi-Neo Village|Q11628785|WD=Q11628785|1=Nishineo}} of Ōno District. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:白山神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:本巣市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} [[File:Shinto shrine Kumanohakusangongen 2010-4-4.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Okumiya (April 2010)]] '''Nōgō Hakusan Shrine''' (のうごうはくさんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Neo Nōgō, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture. Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; {{wikidata link|Q11612076}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Nōgō Hakusan Shrine |Image = [[Image:Shinto shrine Hakusan in Nougou 2010-4-4.JPG|270px|]]
Torii gate and kagura hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Motosu City|Q844047|WD=Q844047|1=Motosu, Gifu}} Neo Nōgō 162 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, debated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Hakusan Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nōgō
Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} [[File:Shinto shrine Kumanohakusangongen 2010-4-4.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Okumiya (April 2010)]] '''Nōgō Hakusan Shrine''' (のうごうはくさんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Neo Nōgō, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture. Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) * Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/motosusi/nougou.html |title=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Gifu Prefecture–designated cultural properties * Japanese bronze mirror (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/wakyou.html |title=Japanese Bronze Mirror |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh masks (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noumenn.html |title=Noh Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen costumes (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noukyougennshou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Costumes |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Bronze temple bell (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/bonnshou.html |title=Bronze Bell |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen manuscripts (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenseki/motosusi/noukyou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Manuscripts |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Offering vessels (Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/motosusi/kumotuki.html |title=Offering Vessels |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Motosu City–designated cultural properties * Buddhist statues{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kaiga/ |title=Paintings and Sculptures |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Lotus Sutra written in gold on indigo paper{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/tenseki/ |title=Manuscripts |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Golden Light Sutra written in gold on indigo paper * Waniguchi gong{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kougei/ |title=Craftworks |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh flute === Access === ; Main shrine * The nearest public transportation is about 10 km from {{ill|lt=Tarumi Station|Q7686918|WD=Q7686918|1=Tarumi Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Q7686917|WD=Q7686917|1=Tarumi Line}} of {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. * Take the Motosu City Bus Nōgō Line and get off at the “Nōgō” bus stop; about a 5-minute walk. === Other === There is a theory that this shrine corresponds to “Hananaga Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Ōno District, Mino Province|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|1=Ōno district}}. Although Nōgō Hakusan Shrine is located in Motosu City (formerly {{ill|lt=Neo Village|Q11536289|WD=Q11536289|1=Neo}} of {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}}), until {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 29 ({{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|WD=Q7846|1=1896}}) it belonged to {{ill|lt=Nishi-Neo Village|Q11628785|WD=Q11628785|1=Nishineo}} of Ōno District. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:白山神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:本巣市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, debated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Hakusan Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nōgō
Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} [[File:Shinto shrine Kumanohakusangongen 2010-4-4.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Okumiya (April 2010)]] '''Nōgō Hakusan Shrine''' (のうごうはくさんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Neo Nōgō, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture. Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) * Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/motosusi/nougou.html |title=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Gifu Prefecture–designated cultural properties * Japanese bronze mirror (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/wakyou.html |title=Japanese Bronze Mirror |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh masks (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noumenn.html |title=Noh Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen costumes (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noukyougennshou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Costumes |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Bronze temple bell (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/bonnshou.html |title=Bronze Bell |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen manuscripts (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenseki/motosusi/noukyou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Manuscripts |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Offering vessels (Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/motosusi/kumotuki.html |title=Offering Vessels |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Motosu City–designated cultural properties * Buddhist statues{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kaiga/ |title=Paintings and Sculptures |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Lotus Sutra written in gold on indigo paper{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/tenseki/ |title=Manuscripts |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Golden Light Sutra written in gold on indigo paper * Waniguchi gong{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kougei/ |title=Craftworks |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh flute === Access === ; Main shrine * The nearest public transportation is about 10 km from {{ill|lt=Tarumi Station|Q7686918|WD=Q7686918|1=Tarumi Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Q7686917|WD=Q7686917|1=Tarumi Line}} of {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. * Take the Motosu City Bus Nōgō Line and get off at the “Nōgō” bus stop; about a 5-minute walk. === Other === There is a theory that this shrine corresponds to “Hananaga Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Ōno District, Mino Province|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|1=Ōno district}}. Although Nōgō Hakusan Shrine is located in Motosu City (formerly {{ill|lt=Neo Village|Q11536289|WD=Q11536289|1=Neo}} of {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}}), until {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 29 ({{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|WD=Q7846|1=1896}}) it belonged to {{ill|lt=Nishi-Neo Village|Q11628785|WD=Q11628785|1=Nishineo}} of Ōno District. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:白山神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:本巣市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} [[File:Shinto shrine Kumanohakusangongen 2010-4-4.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Okumiya (April 2010)]] '''Nōgō Hakusan Shrine''' (のうごうはくさんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Neo Nōgō, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture. Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, debated {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = Hakusan Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = April 13 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Nōgō
Hakusan Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} [[File:Shinto shrine Kumanohakusangongen 2010-4-4.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Okumiya (April 2010)]] '''Nōgō Hakusan Shrine''' (のうごうはくさんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Neo Nōgō, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture. Its {{ill|lt=Okumiya|Q11445659|WD=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} is situated at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Nōgō Hakusan|Q6922468|WD=Q6922468|1=Mount Nōgōhaku}} (elevation 1,617.3 m). Originally, the Okumiya served as the main sanctuary, but the present main shrine was later constructed for the convenience of local residents. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kikuri-hime no Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kikurihime}} (Hakusan-hime no Kami / {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}) * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === History === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}) when {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} transferred the deity from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Hime Shrine|Q704702|WD=Q704702|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kaga Province|Q907469|WD=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}}. It is said to be one of the “Seven Hakusan Shrines of Hokuriku” (another of these shrines is {{ill|lt=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine|Q11434667|WD=Q11434667|1=Ōyama Hakusan Shrine}}). At the time of its founding, it was called “Hakusan Myōri Gongen,” and it is said that {{ill|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|Q416174|WD=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, {{ill|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|Q11404731|WD=Q11404731|lt=Jūichimen Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, and {{ill|lt=Shō Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|lt=Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} were enshrined. In June of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In Meiji 30 ({{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}}), the present shrine buildings were rebuilt, and in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 19 ({{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}}), the main sanctuary underwent restoration. === Festivals and rites === At the annual festival held on April 13, {{ill|lt=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen|Q11612074|WD=Q11612074|1=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen}} are performed. === Shrine buildings === The current main hall dates from the reconstruction of Meiji 30. === Cultural properties === ; Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) * Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/motosusi/nougou.html |title=Nōgō no Noh and Kyōgen |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Gifu Prefecture–designated cultural properties * Japanese bronze mirror (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/wakyou.html |title=Japanese Bronze Mirror |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh masks (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noumenn.html |title=Noh Masks |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen costumes (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/noukyougennshou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Costumes |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Bronze temple bell (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/motosu/bonnshou.html |title=Bronze Bell |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh and Kyōgen manuscripts (Important Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenseki/motosusi/noukyou.html |title=Noh and Kyōgen Manuscripts |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Offering vessels (Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/motosusi/kumotuki.html |title=Offering Vessels |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-24}} ; Motosu City–designated cultural properties * Buddhist statues{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kaiga/ |title=Paintings and Sculptures |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Lotus Sutra written in gold on indigo paper{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/tenseki/ |title=Manuscripts |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Golden Light Sutra written in gold on indigo paper * Waniguchi gong{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.city.motosu.lg.jp/life/kyouiku/culture_asset/kougei/ |title=Craftworks |publisher=Motosu City |accessdate=2013-04-24}} * Noh flute === Access === ; Main shrine * The nearest public transportation is about 10 km from {{ill|lt=Tarumi Station|Q7686918|WD=Q7686918|1=Tarumi Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway Tarumi Line|Q7686917|WD=Q7686917|1=Tarumi Line}} of {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. * Take the Motosu City Bus Nōgō Line and get off at the “Nōgō” bus stop; about a 5-minute walk. === Other === There is a theory that this shrine corresponds to “Hananaga Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} under {{ill|lt=Ōno District, Mino Province|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|1=Ōno district}}. Although Nōgō Hakusan Shrine is located in Motosu City (formerly {{ill|lt=Neo Village|Q11536289|WD=Q11536289|1=Neo}} of {{ill|lt=Motosu District|Q1204331|WD=Q1204331|1=Motosu District, Gifu}}), until {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 29 ({{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|WD=Q7846|1=1896}}) it belonged to {{ill|lt=Nishi-Neo Village|Q11628785|WD=Q11628785|1=Nishineo}} of Ōno District. === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{Reflist|2}} {{Shinto footer}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nōgō Hakusan Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:白山神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:本巣市の建築物]]
